location: Dropped by the boss Rakshasa inside the Eastern Nameless Mausoleum, a small enclosed tomb tucked under a cliff overhang at a waterfall along Recluses' River. From Recluses' River Downstream Site of Grace, follow the river/cliffside path to the mausoleum entrance.
tactics: Do not confuse this with the Western Nameless Mausoleum elsewhere in Scadu Altus — that is a different location entirely; only the Eastern one holds Rakshasa.

Reaching Recluses' River requires dropping down via the coffin/ladder route from Shadow Keep down to Castle Watering Hole, then working south along the river to the Downstream grace before continuing to the mausoleum.

Rakshasa has hyper armor on nearly all her attacks, so light pokes won't stagger her, and her combos can chunk or one-shot low-vigor characters — all attacks except her Leaping Slash are parryable, making a parry-focused approach much safer than trading blows.

She is weak to Lightning and highly resistant to Bleed, Poison, Rot, and Frost, so status-bleed builds underperform here; she cannot be summoned against with Spirit Ashes, though co-op summons are allowed.
lore: The blade's tempering pattern is permanently stained red, marking it as belonging to Rakshasa, a devouring, berserker-like spirit. It is built for a fighting style that shrugs off incoming hits and answers with relentless, sweeping offense rather than careful defense.

builds.name: Pure Dexterity Bleed
builds.statFocus: DEX-heavy (110 DEX scaling, only 12 STR required)
builds.why: Near-total Dexterity scaling with a very low Strength requirement makes it a premier pure-DEX bleed stick, especially since its skill and affinity are fixed and cannot be changed.
